content:
{
  "title": "Fact-Check-Driven Prompt Augmentation for Factuality in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) frequently generate hallucinated content and struggle to maintain factual consistency in open-ended scenarios. Current prompting techniques insufficiently mitigate this factuality deficit.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly integrated into high-stakes applications, ensuring their outputs are factual is critical. Hallucinations undermine trust and limit their safe adoption. Prompting offers a flexible, low-cost approach to guide LLM behavior without costly retraining.",
  "method": "We propose Fact-Check-Driven Prompt Augmentation (FCPA), a prompting method that appends dynamic, context-aware fact-check sub-prompts to the original user query. These sub-prompts instruct the LLM to (1) internally propose a set of candidate facts it is unsure about, (2) self-verify these facts based on its internal knowledge, then (3) explicitly mark any statements in its answer that are likely unverifiable or uncertain. This transparent self-monitoring is achieved by chaining instructions within a single prompt, encouraging the model to separate factual from speculative content during generation.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate FCPA on TruthfulQA and WinoGrande datasets, comparing against standard prompting, chain-of-thought, and retrieval-augmented prompting. Metrics will include factual accuracy (via human and automatic fact-checking), hallucination rate, and answer helpfulness. Ablations will isolate the contributions of each fact-check sub-prompt."
}
